message:
feat(runtime): Add region admission control with configurable limits

Implement backpressure and capacity limiting at the region level to prevent
resource exhaustion and enable flow control in concurrent workloads:

Region Limits Infrastructure:
- Add RegionLimits struct with max_children, max_tasks, max_obligations fields
- Implement AdmissionError enum with Closed and LimitReached variants
- Add AdmissionKind enum to distinguish child/task/obligation rejections
- Export types from record module for public API access

Region Admission Control:
- Change add_child() return type from bool to Result<(), AdmissionError>
- Change add_task() return type from bool to Result<(), AdmissionError>
- Add try_reserve_obligation() for obligation slot reservation with limits
- Add resolve_obligation() to decrement pending count after completion
- Add set_limits() and limits() accessors for runtime configuration
- Track pending_obligations count in RegionInner

Runtime Integration:
- Add SpawnError::RegionAtCapacity variant with region, limit, live fields
- Update RuntimeState::create_task() to handle LimitReached errors
- Update Scope::spawn_internal() to translate admission errors to SpawnError
- Update create_obligation() to return Result and check limits
- Wire resolve_obligation() calls into commit/abort/leak flows
- Add set_region_limits() and region_limits() methods to RuntimeState
- Re-export RegionLimits from runtime module

Error Handling:
- Add ErrorKind::AdmissionDenied for region limit rejections
- Mark as transient with RetryWithBackoff recovery action
- Categorize under ErrorCategory::Region

Observability:
- Fix OTel metric label ownership by cloning task_type to String
- Add missing MeterProvider import in tests

Test Coverage:
- Add region_task_limit_enforced test
- Add region_child_limit_enforced test
- Add region_obligation_limit_enforced test
- Add spawn_rejected_when_task_limit_reached integration test
- Update existing tests for new Result return types

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
